title: ranunculus subfirmicaulis
parts: Ranunculus subfirmicaulis has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain various bioactive compounds. The roots of Ranunculus subfirmicaulis are known to contain glycosides, alkaloids, and flavonoids, including ranunculin, which has been studied for its anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and flowers of the plant are rich in phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant's leaves and stems contain sesquiterpenes and terpenoids, which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Ranunculus subfirmicaulis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Ingestion of the plant's toxic compounds can lead to symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap may cause dermatitis, blisters, and rashes, while allergic reactions can trigger anaphylaxis in severe cases. Improper handling and preparation of the plant may also lead to the release of its toxic compounds, exacerbating its side effects.
